title: Bridge Essay: The "common" experience of voice-hearing and its relationship with shame and gui

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on acontinuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.

**The "common" experience of voice-hearing and its relationship with shame and guilt: asystematic review.**. BMC Psychiatry.. PMID: 35443637. DOI: 10.1186/s12888-022-03902-6. BACKGROUND: Despite Auditory Verbal Hallucinations (AVHs) having been long associated with mental illness, they represent acommon experience also in the non-clinical population, yet do not exhibit distress or need for care. Shame and guilt are emotions related to one'sperception of oneself and one'sresponsibility.
